PROVED This has been solved in the affirmative. - $100
Let $d_n=p_{n+1}-p_n$. Are there infinitely many $n$ such that $d_n<d_{n+1}<d_{n+2}$?
#6: [Er55c] [Er57] [ErGr80]
number theory | primes
Conjectured by Erdős and Turán [ErTu48]. Indeed, the answer is yes, as proved by Banks, Freiberg, and Turnage-Butterbaugh [BFT15].

View the LaTeX source

Formalised statement? Yes
Related OEIS sequences: A335277
